title: Counter Management in Hadoop MapReduce
description:
The 'counters' directory in Hadoop MapReduce architecture is responsible for managing and tracking various counters used during job execution. It comprises several key components:

1. AbstractCounter.java: This abstract class implements the Counter interface, providing common functionality for both mapred and mapreduce packages. It ensures consistent hashing behavior through the implementation of equals and hashCode methods.

2. AbstractCounterGroup.java: This class provides a common implementation for generic counter groups, managing thread-safe operations using ConcurrentSkipListMap. It includes methods for adding, finding, and managing counters within a group, along with serialization and deserialization capabilities.

3. CounterGroupBase.java: This interface defines methods to manage counters within a group, extending Writable and Iterable. It includes functionalities like adding counters, finding existing counters, and incrementing all counters in a group.

4. CounterGroupFactory.java: This abstract class serves as a factory for creating different types of counter groups, using the Abstract Factory design pattern. It maintains mappings between group names and their respective factory classes, ensuring version control and uniqueness.

5. GenericCounter.java: This class extends AbstractCounter, implementing counter functionality for Hadoop MapReduce. It handles reading and writing counter data fields using DataInput and DataOutput.

6. LimitExceededException.java: A custom exception class used to signal that a limit has been exceeded in the context of Hadoop MapReduce counters.

7. Limits.java: This class manages and enforces limits on the number of counter groups and counter names, ensuring these limits are not exceeded and throwing a LimitExceededException if they are.

These components collaborate to provide a robust counter management system, ensuring accurate tracking and reporting of various job-related metrics in Hadoop MapReduce. The architecture follows the Factory Method and Abstract Factory design patterns, promoting code reusability and maintainability.
